architectureChange.decision: All LLM logic moved from Node.js to Python FastAPI service (ai-service/)
architectureChange.rationale: Python is better ecosystem for LLM work — openai/anthropic SDKs, typing, async workers
architectureChange.communication: Node → Python: POST http://ai-service:8000/jobs/dispatch | Python → Node: POST http://backend:3000/api/internal/job-update

completedTasks: ai-service/pyproject.toml — Poetry config with FastAPI, openai, anthropic, google-generativeai
ai-service/main.py — FastAPI app with lifespan, health endpoint, router includes
ai-service/config.py — Pydantic BaseSettings with all required env vars
ai-service/routers/jobs.py — POST /jobs/dispatch with internal secret auth
ai-service/workers/dispatcher.py — jobType → worker routing with Node callback
ai-service/workers/stub_worker.py — generic stub for unimplemented job types
backend/src/services/ai-client.ts — Node HTTP client for Python service
backend/src/services/llm/router.ts — gutted (LLM logic moved to ai-service)
docker-compose.yml — added ai-service container (port 8001 external / 8000 internal)
.env.example — added AI_SERVICE_URL, AI_INTERNAL_SECRET, moved LLM keys note
inProgressTasks: HLD.md update — architecture diagram needs updating to show two services
SPRINTS.md update — Sprint 1.5 tasks need to be formally inserted
CONTEXT.md update — tech stack section needs Python service added

keyDecisions.rationale: Small team, co-deployed services, shared docker-compose — monorepo keeps velocity high
Simpler debugging, Python controls its own concurrency, no BullMQ-Python compatibility headaches

keyDecisions.decision: LLM API keys used by Python only — removed from Node env schema
keyDecisions.rationale: Enforces architectural boundary — Node never calls LLMs directly

keyDecisions.decision: Intake parser + collateral detector are synchronous HTTP calls (not async jobs)
keyDecisions.rationale: These happen in the /message route before agent dispatch — need the result immediately to know what to dispatch

nextAgentInstructions.warden: Review Sprint 1.5 changes. Key things to check: (1) ai-client.ts error handling — if Python service is down, does Node fail gracefully or crash? (2) Internal secret header present on all Python routes? (3) dispatcher.py callback — does it handle httpx errors without crashing the background task? (4) env.ts — AI_SERVICE_URL and AI_INTERNAL_SECRET correctly validated? (5) Docker depends_on ordering correct?
nextAgentInstructions.forge_sprint2: Sprint 2 builds the first real Python agents. Read ai-service/workers/dispatcher.py before starting — add real worker imports there. Sprint 2 workers go in ai-service/workers/: research.py (Secondary Research Agent), context_manager.py (Webknot Context Manager), packaging.py (Packaging Agent for Stage 1+2). All use httpx for web search (Tavily/Brave), openai/anthropic for synthesis. Node BullMQ stub workers already exist — they now call dispatchJob() from ai-client.ts instead of doing LLM work directly.
